steps.title: Find angle UVS
steps.content:
Since $U$, $V$, and $R$ are collinear, angles UVS and RVS form a linear pair:
$$
180^\circ-41^\circ=139^\circ
$$

steps.title: Find angle VUS
steps.content:
Because $U$, $T$, and $S$ are collinear, angle USV equals the given $29^\circ$ angle at $S$. In triangle $UVS$:
$$
180^\circ-139^\circ-29^\circ=12^\circ
$$

steps.title: Use the isosceles triangle
steps.content: In triangle $RTU$, $RT=TU$, so the base angles at $R$ and $U$ are equal. The angle at $U$ is $12^\circ$, so the angle at $R$ is also $12^\circ$: $$ x=180^\circ-12^\circ-12^\circ=156^\circ $$ The answer is 156.
